findings.note: signature compared with '==' (timing attack) and signs str(dict)+header instead of the raw body, so verification is both insecure and unreliable

extension check compares bare ext against dotted list so it always fails closed (blocks every upload); filename still unsanitized if that bug were fixed
verifies password via 'user[1] == hash(password)' — Python's builtin hash() is non-cryptographic, unsalted, and process-randomized, not a password hash

writes the raw upload straight to disk using the unsanitized filename — path traversal / arbitrary write (also never extracts the zip, and 'import os' is missing)

computes a salted PBKDF2 hash but discards it, actually returning an unsalted SHA-256 hash of the raw password
